description: The retry routing action (onFail:[{retry:{limit,delay,backoff}}]). Only the no-op permutation is expressible as a PASS/SKIPPED fixture (a passing step never retries, proving retry is opt-in and additive). The stateful/FAIL-path behaviors — retry recovering a transient failure, retry exhausting to the default stop, and retry-then-continue — are asserted in focused it() tests in core-core.test.js, since they need a counter file or resolve to FAIL. Cross-platform via `node -e`.

tests.description: A passing step carrying onFail:[{retry:{limit:3,delay:0}}] never fails, so the retry never fires and the step PASSes exactly as if no handler were set (additive/byte-identical).

steps.description: Passes on the first attempt; retry is never reached.

tests.description: Same no-op with an exponential-backoff retry spec, exercising schema validation of the full retry shape (limit + delay + backoff) on a passing step.

steps.description: Passes; the retry (exponential backoff) is never reached.
